entry.title: Re: Embracing Sustainability with SAP Ariba
entry.content:
For inputs on Sustainable Procurement with reference to BRSR (Business Responsibility and Sustainability Reporting) in India, kindly refer this interesting article.
Impact of Sustainable Procurement in ESG

entry.title: Same LTMC Staging table throughout the SAP Environments
entry.content:
Hello,

Did anyone across a scenario where we have multiple SAP systems and we have to work with LTMC staging tables. The issue is the name of staging tables get changed in another sap system for same object.

Because of the different table name, we will be having difficulties manages the tables in BODS. Because if we want load data in another sap system, we will need to change the table. For example if we have 10 SAP systems then 10 different staging tables for the same object and we will have to manage those 10 tables each time we execute the BODS job.

Can anyone please explain if you have come across this scenario.

Thanks,

Imran

entry.title: How to keep track of graduates in SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Hi experts,

We want to track our graduates in SuccessFactors. Who are they, how long do they work in our company, which positions do they get after being a graduate and so on. As far as I know, there aren't any standard solutions for this. 

Any recommendations? 

Br,

Ines

entry.title: Re: In FBCJ only 1 partcular profit center to be accepted...
entry.content: Perform first some search on FI validation rule

entry.title: Bulk Download Onboarding 1.0 Documents
entry.content: What is the best way to extract all documents out of ONB 1.0? We have enabled the bulk download feature but the zip files are not user friendly as you don't know who the new hire is until you open the zip file and look at the files. It appears we are unable to export all files if we do an unfiltered search and bulk download to SFTP.  I looked at using postman to utilize the API but we are limited to 100 files per pull. Looking for recommendations on how to best export all documents for all new hires.

entry.title: Error oData Iflow in CPI - java.util.ArrayList cannot be cast to java.util.HashMap
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I have an iflow with oData service in CPI, and when consume with Postman, I have the following error when the response returns multilple records:

java.lang.ClassCastException: java.util.ArrayList (loaded by <bootstrap>) cannot be cast to java.util.HashMap (loaded by <bootstrap>)

Any suggest what may be happening?

Attached EDMX model and xml response.

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Recalculation of header conditions when cancelling a...
entry.content: Hello Raymond, thank you for your reply. yes we actually saw that but did not find what we needed to. We actually found a solution. The key is to enhance the cancellation reasons with an "X" value at Statistics.

entry.title: Import/Export Route from Dev/QA/Prod
entry.content:
Hello 

Would like to know how the depoyment in Datasphere works, if there are Dev - QA - Prod Tenant. 

Dev - Export Package_A will be created 
QA - In Import Queue, we will see the corresponding Export Package_A, and deployment will be done.
Prod - What are the Options? 

Can we still use the same Export Package_A ? if yes ? how to use the same Package_A and import to Prod ? 

Manualy Export is the only option from QA to Prod ??

entry.title: Re: Packaging Specification is not determined after Inbou...
entry.content:
PLEASE CHECK whether the following configuration has been maintained in your system

SPRO-> SAP Extended Warehouse Management -> Goods Receipt process -> Inbound delivery -> Existence check of packaging specification -> Activate Existence Check of Packaging Specification.

entry.title: Find Release Information & Content with the Release Navigator for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
If you’re a customer using SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, you won’t want to miss our upcoming virtual session on the Release Navigator offered by the SAP Enterprise Support Academy. This powerful tool is designed to simplify the process of finding release information and content, allowing you to stay informed and prepared for upcoming updates.

Join us for an interactive virtual live session where we’ll walk you through the features and benefits of the Release Navigator. Discover how this tool provides a comprehensive view of upcoming releases, allowing you to easily plan and prepare for updates. You’ll also learn how to find release scheduling and maintenance windows within the release navigator, enabling you to better plan for and manage the deployment of updates.

But that’s not all – we’ll also show you how to simplify the release process by utilizing consolidated content from SAP experts found inside the release navigator. This means you can access all the information you need in one centralized platform, saving you time and effort when it comes to staying on top of the latest updates and enhancements.

Whether you’re new to the release navigator or looking to enhance your existing knowledge, this virtual session is for you. Our experienced presenters will provide valuable insights and practical tips that you can immediately apply to your daily operations.

Don’t miss out on this opportunity to take your release management to the next level. Register now to secure your spot in the upcoming virtual session and unlock the full potential of the Release Navigator for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. We look forward to seeing you there!

Please note:
Before you can access the link to the Meet-the-Expert session, a one-time registration in SAP Learning Hub, edition for SAP Enterprise Support, is required. A detailed step-by step guide to registration can be found here.

 Register here for October 23 - 10am EST:

Find Release Information & Content with the Release Navigator for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

entry.title: Custom List entity using REUSE mode in sap MDG-S?
entry.content:
We have requirement to add Custom list entity using Reuse mode in MDG-S. we have the data mode extensions, genil Extensions and SMT mapping and brought Custom ENTITY in MDG UI. 

 

Issue is : - when we entered the data inside the custom entity UIBB. once Change request is Activated then data which we entered in the UIBB is getting Vanished and not getting updated in the Custom database table.

 

Kindly Help with Solution and Links.

 

Thanks in Advance

best Answer will be Rewarded.

Thanks for writing! Take a look at this blog for guidance.

You've got an invite in your private messages.

Regards,

David

entry.title: Re: RefCount Error When Adding UserField Using SAP Busine...
entry.content:
Hi JonathasCosta,

To avoid this error please ensure that no other object are running during the addition of the user defined fields. When you are trying to update table/field in the database, you should kill all the running objects and use only the object that the update. You should also set the User Defined Table/User Defined Field to be equal to nothing at the end of the procedure.

In case of DIAPI

 

oUFields = oCompany.GetBusinessObject(SAPbobsCOM.BoObjectTypes.oUserFields)

'Add the field code .....................

'IMPORTANT: Release the object
System.Runtime.InteropServices.Marshal.ReleaseComObject(oUFields)

'Then set object variable to Nothing
oUFields = Nothing

 

Kind regards,

ANKIT CHAUHAN

SAP Business One Support

entry.title: Re: Certification exam validity
entry.content: Hi @Ritesh911 , After you redeem your campaign code, you will have 12 months to schedule & take your exam.

entry.title: Re: SAP Service Layer Missing Many Columns
entry.content:
Hi connerpanarella,

Not all the fields are guaranteed to be accessible in the DI API or Service Layer.

You have the option to create a DRQ within the SAP Business One Customer Influence site for the fields that you believe are necessary.

Kind regards,

ANKIT CHAUHAN

SAP Business One Support

entry.title: Finding Attachment Document Locations in Solman ChaRM
entry.content:
I'm working with Solman ChaRM and trying to determine the exact location where attachment documents are stored within the system. I've searched through the documentation and configuration settings, but I haven't been able to pinpoint the specific directory or database table where these documents are located.

I'm interested in knowing the location so that I can:

  • Access and download attachments directly: Without going through the ChaRM interface.
  • Perform manual backups: Of important attachments .

If anyone has experience or knowledge about finding attachment document locations in Solman ChaRM, I would greatly appreciate your assistance. Please share any relevant information, tips, or best practices that you may have.

Thank you!

SOLMAN Change Control Management  SOLMAN IT Service Management SOLMAN Diagnostics

entry.title: database connection to Microsoft Dynamics 365
entry.content: Been using Crystal reports for decades on local SQL servers. But now we are moving to cloud based Microsoft Dynamics 365 Business Central. Guess I am looking in wrong places but I cannot find how to create a connection to the data source.  Have the trial version of SAP Crystal reports loaded

entry.title: We wish to use AP to assign items that have previously been completed.
entry.content:
We are trying to set up the following training pattern...

1. User completes training course which auto assigns step 2.

2. At 2 years Manager confirms that user has continued good practice which auto assigns step 3.

3. User must complete refresher item within 1 year which then auto assigns back step 2.

This is then ongoing.

When building the process, we get to step 3 (all assignment profiles working as required) but cannot auto assign back to step 2.

Any ideas

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to run a program or background job only on individual PC?
entry.content: Based on your input, I don't see a single reason to develop the program in ABAP. Develop a program in the language you want, install it on the user's laptop, and that's it.

entry.title: What ia an Enterprise Architect (EA) and what are the core aspects of enterprise architecture?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Discovering SAP Enterprise Architecture Portfolio learning journey and explore more.

Overview
The Discovering the Enterprise Architect course provides SAP professionals with a comprehensive introduction to the role of an Enterprise Architect (EA) within the SAP ecosystem. It covers the essential fundamentals for success, focusing on the core aspects of enterprise architecture. Participants will explore key EA responsibilities and gain insight into foundational concepts, including critical artefacts, guiding principles, and reference architecture.

Learning objectives

Upon completing this course, you will:

  1. Understand the Role of an EA:
    Grasp the key responsibilities of an Enterprise Architect in aligning IT with business goals within SAP.
  2. Master EA Fundamentals:
    Learn essential methodologies, concepts and principles crucial for success as an EA.
  3. Explore EA Artefacts and Principles:
    Gain insight into core artefacts and principles that support effective enterprise architecture in SAP.
  4. Understand Reference Architecture:
    Develop an understanding of SAP’s reference architecture and its role in designing scalable IT solutions.

Prerequisites

  • Exploring SAP’s Cloud Strategy

Pass all the quizzes and receive a digital badge

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area.

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Implementation of e-invoicing and reporting mandates in SAP Document and Reporting Compliance
entry.content:
Would you like to refresh your knowledge on SAP Document and Reporting Compliance implementation?

We are inviting you to the one-and-a-half-hour virtual session that will be mainly interesting for individuals in the role of 'consultant' from the Customer, Partner, or SAP teams.

In this session we will provide an overview of the involved components and implementation / configuration required to get the SAP Document and Reporting Compliance framework ready.

When: Wednesday, October 9, 2024

CEST (UTC +2)  03:00 p.m.

BST (UTC +1)  02:00 p.m.

EDT (UTC -4)  09:00 a.m.

BRT (UTC -3)  10:00 a.m.

IST (UTC +5:30)  06:30 p.m.

Registration link

entry.title: Re: Can you explicitly Clear or reset HANA NSE Buffer cache?
entry.content: I do not see "indexserver.ini -> [buffer_cache_cs] -> enabled" in Note 2799997. In fact I can't find the "enabled" parameter anywhere is SAP documentation. Where did you read this?

entry.title: Re: Task 3 - Enhancing CDS View Entities with Annotations
Re: C4C How to display visits in campaigns
entry.content:
Hello,

I faced the same issue,but with a different customer.

WIhtin the activites tab of the sales campaign you can still not easily add a standard visit section, although it seems from a business process point of view quite common to reference sales campaings in visits.

So indeed, i created a visit report view  within a standard visit analyses report or any other report that contains visit details including the 'preceeding campaign' field.

Via Adaptation within the Sales Campaign, Tab: Ativities, i defined an embedded report, selected my report view and added  Campaign(*) in the Selection and Filter area.

It works ok

Kr. MJ

entry.title: Re: Questions regarding Sucessfactors - Common super doma...
entry.content:
Hi @karthik1gobburu 

Migration to CSD was required only because Google had announced deprecation of support for 3rd party cookies. However, in July Google has cancelled this deprecation, meaning migration to CSD is currently not mandatory. Check out this KBA:

3459706 - Enablement of Common Super Domain Migration

https://me.sap.com/notes/0003459706

As for IAS, I am not sure. But IAS is a prerequisite for Report Story and also a better integration between BizX and LMS so it is something all customers should do, sooner rather than later.

Best regards

Erik Ebert - Effective People

entry.title: Re: Task 4 - Implement Criticality feature and create an Object page
Re: Entering batch at the time of Putaway - EWM
entry.content:
Please refer to the below SAP Note under the reason and prerequisites section for more details of the system behavior.

 

3421025 - Editing of Vendor Batch Number in EWM Inbound Delivery for not batch managed materials

Symptom

Until now it is not possible to edit the field Vendor Batch in the EWM inbound delivery for materials which are not batch managed. This note is enabling this missing standard functionality in the EWM system.

Some customers require the maintenance of the Vendor Batch field for certain components due to traceability reasons. For these materials, the Vendor Batch field has to be maintained in the EWM Inbound delivery before the goods receipt posting.

Other Terms

Extended Warehouse Management, EWM, Vendor Batch

Reason and Prerequisites

Inbound deliveries are created in advance based on the ASN and are distributed to EWM. In case the Vendor Batch was not included in the ASN, the Vendor Batch has to be maintained in the EWM Inbound delivery during the registration of goods receipt.

The materials are not batch managed, there is no batch created during the goods receipt process.

The feature works with unpacked items as well as already (pre-)packed items in the LE system which are distributed to EWM.

entry.title: Time Sheet: Approved time sheet should not be changed by emps (Only Admin/Manager should)
entry.content:
Hi,

For this requirement, we need to add below Save rule (Approved_timesheet_donot_change) in object: Employee Time Sheet

Rule: Approved_timesheet_donot_change

entry.title: Re: Task 4 - Implement Criticality feature and create an Object page
entry.content: My submission for Task 4 - Object page displaying the details of the selected record including Criticality representation of the Status field.

entry.title: Re: Users active in BizX but to be made inactive in LMS -...
entry.content:
Hi @annasergi 

This is a tricky question and not easy to answer in a thread like this. I would recommend you seek advice from an experienced LMS & IAS specialist. There are several dependencies to factor into the design of your scenario

As a general advice, you should be careful about not syncing over a user from BizX to LMS, for example if a manager is not synched and some of her direct reports are, and they request a course for approval - who would approve if the manger is not there? 

It sounds like you have done quite some thinking about it, these are a couple of KBAs on the topic I can recommend. 

2668004 - BizX to LMS User Connector - SF Troubleshooting

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/E/2668004

3302924 - [SF IAS] Sync between LMS / ONB / Bizx modules | How IAS handles user's personal and professional emails when learning module is used before hiring, during onboarding and after

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3302924

Best regards,

Erik Ebert - Effective People

entry.title: Need to add F4 help to fields created using Screen Exit
entry.content:
Hi all, 

For CO02 transaction added 3 custom fields using SCREEN-EXIT. Now for each field I need F4 help, when I tried to assign F4 help using F4IF_INT_TABLE_ON_VALUE_REQUEST not able to get values on screen. 

Is these any other steps I need to follow to add F4 help to field created using screen exit

entry.title: Re: Migration roadmap from ECC to S4/HANA with two SIDs
entry.content:
Hello @KelberMonteiro,

Like Jose replied this is indeed supported by SAP. However, I think that it is important to ask the following question:

Is there still a need to have 2 system ID´s (instances)? (I have also seen this architecture often, but in max 50% of the cases this architecture was really needed)

  • If yes,
    • Conversion at the same moment or on different moments?
  • If no,
    • What is the impact on the brownfield approach?
    • Which system will become the master ? (without knowing the complete context I would say that ISU is the master and that this one could be migrated via a brownfield approach)

Good luck with your study. Curious about the outcomes of it.

Regards,

Pieter

entry.title: Single dunning letter for multiple company codes at same dunning level
entry.content:
Hi Team,

Requirement is to print all open items on single dunning notice from multiple company codes which has same level of dunning level.

Given i have maintained all the required cross-company code configuration, still system is printing separate dunning letters for each company code at same dunning level. If anyone can suggest, will be great help. 

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Task 2 - CDS View Entity Associations ( August Developer Challenge )
Single order staging process flow
entry.content:
There are two types of staging processes namely:

1)      Single order staging

2)      Cross order staging

 

1) Single Order Staging:

Single Order Staging is a logistics and supply chain management concept where the process of preparing and staging inventory for a single order is executed. This process involves gathering, organizing, and preparing the items specified in a single customer order before they are dispatched for delivery. Single Order Staging is particularly important for order fulfillment and ensures that each order is processed accurately and efficiently. In SAP Extended Warehouse Management (EWM), single-order staging allows users to request that components be staged for a specific order. This involves moving components from a warehouse to a production supply area (PSA).

 

2) Cross Order Staging:

Cross Order Staging is a logistics strategy used to optimize the handling and preparation of multiple orders simultaneously. Unlike Single Order Staging, which focuses on preparing one order at a time, Cross Order Staging involves organizing and staging items from multiple orders together in a way that can improve efficiency and reduce overall handling time. For cross-order staging, the system proposes a quantity for staging for each component that is required on the PSA. The system considers only PMRs with the status Released. You can create warehouse tasks for the replenishment with a different quantity to the quantity proposed by the system.

 

Process Order:

A Process Order is a term commonly used in manufacturing and production environments, particularly in industries that use process-based production methods, such as chemical, pharmaceutical, or food and beverage industries. It refers to a detailed plan or instruction set for producing a specific quantity of a product, typically involving multiple steps or stages in the production process.

 

PMR:

In SAP Extended Warehouse Management (SAP EWM), a production material request (PMR) is a document that contains information about the materials required for a manufacturing order. This information includes the products, quantities, production supply areas, and dates for which the materials will be staged. The PMR is created in SAP EWM after a manufacturing order is created and released in the ERP system. The PMR acts as a link between production planning and extended warehouse management, and is essential for efficient production.

 

Staging:

Staging refers to the process of preparing and organizing goods or materials in a designated area or staging location within a warehouse before they are either shipped out to customers, used in production, or moved to another location within the warehouse. Staging is a crucial step in warehouse management and inventory handling as it helps to streamline operations, improve efficiency, and ensure that goods are ready for the next phase in the supply chain.

 

Single Order Staging Process Flow:

Process Order:

Create the Process Order using the transaction code COR1

Provide the below details and save the Process order:

a)      Materials

b)      Basic dates (start and end)

 

 

PMR:

Navigate to T-code /N/SCWM/PMR to open Maintain Prd Material Request.

Click the drop down in Find and select Manufacturing order.

Enter Process Order.

Click the Execute button.

PMR Document will be displayed.

Verify PMR for Process Orders- Item and header details for correctness like staging method, product, qty, bin, PSA, Process type for Single order staging

 

Staging:

Navigate to /N/SCWM/STAGE to schedule staging for production

Provide the below details:

1)      Warehouse number

2)      Staging method

3)      Manufacturing Order

De-select Test mode checkbox and then click on the execute button.

 

Five warehouse tasks are created.

Navigate to the transaction code /SCWM/MON and then go to Documents. Warehouse tasks

Five warehouse tasks are Confirmed in the Background.

 

Conclusion:

The Process flow steps for Single Order Staging is provided.

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to run a program or background job only on individual PC?
entry.content:
It's a portal of 'tax service'.

When company sell something, it's need to provide information about deal(to 'tax service'), the buyer should  confirm this document about the deal.

So these document are about, what our company have bought.

I need to download the document, compare with information in sap system and confirm it, if values are similar.

entry.title: SAC - Widget with separate filters
entry.content:
Hi All,

I have this report:

The measure "Volume spedito" in the table is filtered by the date in green.

I need to create another KPI of "Volume Spedito" that depends only by an other filter, for example or the attribute "Anno-Mese Spedito".

So the final result is that the measure "Volume spedito" in the table is filtered by the filter in green, and the  measure "Volume spedito", only value as numeric point, is filtered by other dimensions.

The user will choice different filter for the table and different filter for the numeric point.

I know It's possible to do it making 2 pages, and creating filters of the pages, but the perfect report is in only one page.

 

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere: BW Series – Getting Started

entry.title: Re: Streamline Your PPAP Journey with SAP PLM Collaboration
entry.content: Excellent and very helpful write-up! Thanks Ayan!

entry.title: SF Time Sheet: Time sheet should not allow to record data more than 30days in advance
entry.content:
Hi,

option 1) This validation rule can be done in Time recording profile, under 'Validation Rules' 

option 2) we need to add similar rule (Stop_Save_Submit_Futuredated_30days) as above in 'Save rule' in object: Employee Time Sheet

Rule: Stop_Save_Submit_Futuredated_30days

Hope I am clear to everyone. Thanks.

entry.title: SAP BO WebI Cross table with one value on different dates
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I have two queries, my dimensions are date, customer_id, group.
Each customer can switch between groups over time, so at date 1.1.2024 he is group A and 1.6.2024 he is group C.
So I have one query selecting the first date, and the other one is selecting the other. 

Now, I want to show this changing in a table, with all customers counted and how they changed over time, basically with an output like this:

This table shows that we had 50 customers in group A at both dates at the same time, from these 50 customers, 13 moved into group B at 01.06.2024 and so on.

My result always looks like this, I cant get the numbers to show on the whole table.

Hope you can help me.

Best regards

entry.title: Local vs overseas vendor
entry.content: Is there any difference between the payable processing of Local vendor and that of Overseas Vendor? A separate chart of account has been created for overseas vendor. But we do not know the underlying reasons.

entry.title: Missing Web Recorder Videos
entry.content:
One of my Superusers created some videos using Web Recorder in Enable Now Manager and used them for delivering Info sessions. She has the appropriate browser extension for doing this task. They worked fine for some time and then 3 of them stopped working for some reason and now this is what shows up in Manager for the Groups that used to contain those videos (below screenshot). She confirmed that she did not delete or move the videos. The first video under the group What is Fiori called Fiori Overview was also created using Web Recorder and it is still working normally.

The way I understand the icon appearing next to the groups having worked in Producer is that it usually appears when content objects are not downloaded from server and synchronizing would resolve the issue. But since these objects are already in the Manager (server), I do not understand the root cause and they were working at one point.

I have the Author's link for those videos since the Superuser inserted them in her presentation and I noted that the UID has a much longer string than what we have in Producer (16 characters) for a project. Here is an example - (project!PR_A27F76BDF81148699D324AC4A2247C49). When she clicks on the link within the presentation to launch the video below is error message that she is getting -

I tried to troubleshoot in Manager and could not find the videos in Unsorted or Trash and there is no trace of them. Could this have happened due to the new cloud version that occurred right around the time this issue was reported to me?

SAP Enable Now 

Please advise.

entry.title: Re: Task 4 - Implement Criticality feature and create an Object page
Percent of custom field for budget, budget still shows for employees that are ineligble
entry.content:
Hi

For salary raises our plan has a budget using a "percent of custom field" in my compensation plan. It all works fine except this one case. In the eligibility rules there are certain employees that are not eligible for a recommendation or budget for salary. The field appears greyed out as expected and no recommendation can be entered for them but the budget still appears for them. This isn't the case for my cost of living increase that is a budget using a guideline. 

Salary raise budget

  • The radio button for the salary raise budget advanced options is set to "Include ineligible" - No
  • custom field for the budget still calculates a value for these employees (I thought this would get ignored though as these people aren't eligible)
  • budget is "based on"   template 

What am I missing?

entry.title: Purchase order date
entry.content:
Hello experts,


I have the following question regarding the purchase order date.


The purchase order date is the creation date, which is also the date that appears on the purchasing document sent to the supplier.
My customer wants the PO approval date to appear on the document sent to the supplier, instead of the PO creation date.
In other words, the purchase order should be sent with the date on which it was approved, not the date on which it was created.


Is there a way to do this?


Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Re: Packaging Specification is not determined after Inbound Delivery is distributed to EWM
entry.content:
Hi Team,

Please check Partner Role and PackMatPropProc is maintained or not.

Path - SCM Extended Warehouse Management > Goods Receipt Process > Inbound Delivery > Define Document Types for Inbound Delivery Process 

Check under the document type INB.  Please update partner Role and PackMatPropProc if missing.

entry.title: PT62 equivalent for data between two dates
entry.content:
I'm investigating clock-in/out time event data across our organisation. We have a custom report but I've run into data issues where it omits clock-in/out data (P10/P20 Time Event data) if an individual has entries in CAT2 as well as clock data (can be viewed in PTMW and PT62). 

PT62 seems to give the information I need, and can be run for multiple Pers Nos. However, PT62 only runs for a single key date rather than a full week/month. Another alternative I looked at was whether Ad Hoc Query could extract P10/P20 Time Event data, however it seems it has not been configured to allow that. 

 Is there an alternative or equivalent to PT62 that I can run for all P10/P20 data points between two dates?

entry.title: Re: error while copying purchase contract using manage purchase contract app
entry.content: How did you solve this error - Transaction canceled AM 840 ( ADR13 )

entry.title: Re: In production order origin and origin text are not shown when unplanned issue of goods is done?
entry.content: Hello Jose, did you ever found an explanation for why the system was giving you without origin even when costing 1 was checked?

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to run a program or background job only on individual PC?
entry.content:
The non-ABAP program can easily call an RFC-enabled function module to query data.

Another solution is that the user starts an ABAP program in SAP GUI, which displays a screen, and this screen automatically runs ABAP code every XXX seconds via CL_GUI_TIMER.

entry.title: Re: My 'other' Hobby - Hedgehog Handler - taking care of garden 'wildlife' down under...
entry.content: Reminds me rescuing this pure creature 🦔

entry.title: Re: Need to add F4 help to fields created using Screen Exit
entry.content: Please don't post to Questions about SAP Websites (issue with community Website, etc.) if it's a question about SAP software. Instead, ask in Technology Q&A: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/qa-p/technology-questions. Hopefully someone will fix it for you.

entry.title: Re: Need to add F4 help to fields created using Screen Exit
entry.content: It should work. No problem for me.

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to run a program or background job only on individual PC?
entry.content:
I don't understand why i cant use abap.

As i told with dongle everything is works fine. 

I'm getting the documents from portal and can compare them, and can confirm.

But the problem is, that user need to start a program manually.

Hello community,

3505579 – W-2 / 1099-R Forms, Envelopes and PR Approval letter for year 2024

Graziela

entry.title: Re: SAP BUILD APPS Showing Blank page after login in SAP...
entry.content:
I would assume you do not have any Registry role. Strangely, if you do not have that role it just opens a blank page, with no error message.

These are the roles within the Build_Admin role collection:

You need the Registry to access the SAP Build Lobby.

entry.title: Re: Need help to get Build-Apps ressources
entry.content:
Maybe expand on what you need more help as, since I do not think Build Apps really has a significant expert level when it comes to variables and logic -- some tips/tricks and some quirks you have to get used to (like all development tools).

You can look at this 2-minute video playlist: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ZpQM2B1v2GY&list=PL6RpkC85SLQA9u5lU-xobZNJp-Fw41I57

This is a little more advanced tutorial: https://developers.sap.com/mission.sap-build-apps-social-media.html

And here is how to create S/4HANA CRUD operations: https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/build-apps-s4hana-crud.html

Currently there are no templates, but they are coming soon.

HTH,

Daniel

entry.title: Re: Task 4 - Implement Criticality feature and create an Object page
entry.content:
Hi @Shilpa_Shankar 
Task-4: Object page with a criticality representation of the Status field.

Regards,
Chandana.

entry.title: Using Signavio Process Governance Event Data in Process Intelligence
entry.content:
Curious if anyone is using Process Governance Event Data in Process Intelligence.  The current analytics capability in Process Governance is good for simple reporting, but we've started exporting event data and importing into Process Intelligence for further analysis and dashboarding.  As well, once in PI, we can use live insights to enhance the process diagram that workflows would originally built off of.

Today, it is all a manual effort of moving the data, which results in us just doing monthly, vs. having something closer to daily / real-time.  Would be great if in the future we could automatically bring data into PI from Process Governance, similar to leveraging APIs to bring in data from external sources.

entry.title: Re: Purchase order date
entry.content:
Hi 

For this requirement, you need to give logic.

To find Release date Go to SE16n, enter CDHDR / CDPOS & enter PO Value and use the object class EINKBELEG & Tcode as Me28 to Me29n.

Give reference of this date 

Regards

Rashid Khan

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Apps Access User Identity Token
entry.content:
I'm not really an expert here, but when the backend needs to know the user, we usually use a destination with UserTokenExchange, and the current BTP user is sent so as long as the backend accepts the BTP user all is fine.

Or are you making a separate request to get the user token?

entry.title: Re: Save Data from a CSV file
entry.content: How are you "saving" the data? What is the full flow? Screenshots always help 😺

entry.title: Integrating SAP S/4HANA Invoicing with KSeF Using SAP Event Mesh and CAP
entry.content:
This blog post aims to propose an example on how SAP Business Events can be used for integrating SAP S/4HANA systems with KSeF.

The National System of e-Invoices (KSeF) is a new document exchange platform introduced in Poland. All invoices, including those generated in SAP systems, must be registered in KSeF starting from February 2026.

Architecture overview

The solution architecture relies on SAP Business Events and the Cloud Application Programming Model. When an invoice is created, a business event is triggered and handled by a CAP application. The necessary invoice data is then retrieved through an OData API. Once all the required information is gathered, the invoice is sent to KSeF via the KSeF API.

Billing Document Events enabling

Enterprise Event Enablement is a technical component in SAP S/4HANA that facilitates event-driven communication across different platforms. It enables seamless event exchange by providing a wide range of standard business events (a comprehensive list of these events can be found on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub).

In this context, we are particularly interested in Billing Documents Events. Whenever a Billing Document is created, changed, or deleted, a standard event is triggered. To consume this event in our CAP application, we need to configure an event channel to our SAP Event Mesh instance (detailed guides are available for both Cloud and OnPremise configurations). Once the configuration is complete, you can consume events in your SAP Event Mesh instance.

Billing Document Created event consumed in SAP Event Mesh

Building a CAP application for event consumption 

The next step is to build a CAP application in the SAP BTP environment that will consume events raised by your S/4HANA system. We are leveraging the Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP) because it integrates well with SAP Advanced Messaging. A detailed guide on how to consume events in your CAP application can be found in this tutorial.

Once the CAP application is set up, it will automatically receive information whenever an invoice is created in the system.


Created invoice in SAP system


Created invoice in CAP Application

To register an invoice in KSeF, we need various details such as invoice data and customer information. Unfortunately, SAP's standard business events are merely notification events—they simply alert us when an invoice is created. To retrieve additional information, we must request it using the OData API.

Billing Documents and Business Partner API enabling

To consume OData services from your CAP application, follow these steps:

  • Activate OData Service in S/4HANA system
  • Create Technical User with appropriate roles
  • Configure connection between your system and SAP BTP account
  • Add Service to your CAP application 

You can find the detailed guide here.

Once the necessary OData services are enabled, you can gather all required information and send your invoice via the KSeF API.


The invoice is sent to KSeF


The invoice is successfully registered in KSeF


The invoice is available in the KSeF app

This is an practical example of how to easily integrate your S/4HANA system with KSeF using SAP Event Mesh and the Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP). SAP Event Mesh enables smooth communication between systems through events, while CAP efficiently manages and processes those events. This setup automates the flow of invoice data and provides a flexible and scalable solution that can adapt to future needs.

entry.title: Recruit-to-Hire with multiple payComponents
entry.content:
Hi Experts, 

I’m seeking advice on implementing recurring pay components from Recruiting to Employee Central. I attempted to map all relevant pay components and the amounts using the Recruit-to-Hire mapping. However, it appears that the amount field is required when a pay component is added. In my case, not like the base salary, the allowance-type pay components are not applicable to all candidates, which could cause the integration to fail.

If allowances should not be integrated via the Recruit-to-Hire mapping, what would be the best approach to push this data to Employee Central? Thank you in advance for your guidance!

entry.title: Data Templates for Concur Data Delivery Service (DDS) : POLICY COMPLIANCE SAC Story
entry.content:
Overview

The Data Templates for the Concur Data Delivery Service (DDS) Expense content package offers a powerful solution for visualizing and analyzing data captured in SAP Concur Expense. Seamlessly integrated with SAP Data Delivery Services and utilizing the SAP Analytics Cloud for data visualization, this solution provides administrators with comprehensive insights into organizational expenses, system administration, and user distributions. Its advanced analytics capabilities include robust auditing and control features essential for fraud detection and compliance assurance.

Built upon Analytical Models in SAP Datasphere and visualized in SAP Analytics Cloud, this content package encompasses a suite of detailed stories that deliver in-depth perspectives on expense management. These insights empower administrators to make informed decisions, optimize expense workflows, and enhance overall financial governance.

The Policy Compliance narrative centers on spotting and handling expense anomalies to maintain adherence to policies. Utilizing visual tools and insights, it facilitates the early detection and resolution of policy breaches, thereby strengthening financial governance and compliance efforts.

 

Policy Compliance

This dashboard provides a detailed summary of your users' adherence to company policies by monitoring expense exceptions. It enables you to assess whether users and approvers correctly understand and follow the policy. By examining various metrics, you can implement corrective actions, such as reaching out to users or adjusting the policy as needed. An input control feature allows you to toggle between exceptions related to entire claims or individual expenses.

  • Number of Exceptions: Total of Exceptions attached to expenses.
  • Exception Volume / Amount Over Time: Revealing the evolution of exceptions over time.
  • Top Policy Exceptions: Providing a top exceptions analysis, in term of amount, occurrences and number of employees.
  • Top Employees with Highest No of Exceptions: identifying users with the highest number of exceptions.
  • Top Expenses with Highest No of Exceptions: Providing a ranking of the Expense Types with most exceptions attached to them.
  • Number of Exceptions by Approval Status: displaying the exceptions’ repartition by Expense Reports statuses.
  • Exceptions details: The exhaustive list and breakdown of all occurrences.

Resources:

  • https://community.sap.com/t5/spend-management-blogs-by-sap/data-templates-for-dds-expense-administra...
  • https://community.sap.com/t5/spend-management-blogs-by-sap/data-templates-for-concur-data-delivery-e...
  • https://www.concur.com/data-delivery-service
  • https://api.sap.com/package/SAPConcurExpenseDDSIntegrationwithSAPDatasphere/overview

entry.title: Re: How can I retrieve the displayed value of a field like `KNA1-LIFNR`, or in cases like `DFKKBPTAX
entry.content: I'm unable to retrieve the values for DFKKBPTAXNUM-TAXTYPE and DFKKBPTAXNUM-TAXNUMXL using the code: ls_dynfields-fieldname = 'DFKKBPTAXNUM-TAXTYPE'. APPEND ls_dynfields TO dynfields. CLEAR ls_dynfields. ls_dynfields-fieldname = 'DFKKBPTAXNUM-TAXNUMXL'. APPEND ls_dynfields TO dynfields. CALL FUNCTION 'DYNP_VALUES_READ' EXPORTING dyname = 'SAPLBUPA_BUTX_DIALOG' " Nombre del programa dynumb = '0100' " Número de pantalla (dynpro) translate_to_upper = 'X' DETERMINE_LOOP_INDEX = 'X' SEARCH_OWN_SUBSCREENS_FIRST = 'X' SEARCHPATH_OF_SUBSCREEN_AREAS = 'X' TABLES dynpfields = dynfields EXCEPTIONS invalid_abapworkarea = 1 invalid_dynprofield = 2 invalid_dynproname = 3 invalid_dynpronummer = 4 invalid_request = 5 no_fielddescription = 6 invalid_parameter = 7 undefind_error = 8 double_conversion = 9 stepl_not_found = 10 others = 11.

entry.title: Re: Nota SAP para condição de pagamento acima de 999 dias (OBB8)
entry.content: Dear Good afternoon! The page is unavailable. You will be able to make this installment over 999 days
